steps.title: Identify the circle's center and radius
steps.content: From the standard form $(x-6)^2+(y-2)^2=36$, we have center $(6, 2)$ and radius $6$.

steps.title: Substitute the point into the circle equation and solve
Confirm the answer
steps.content: The final check is to make sure the computed value answers exactly what the question asks for. The work above gives $2$, and substituting or interpreting that value in the original condition satisfies the requirement. Therefore the value to enter is $2$.
